meta.name: Create manager tasks from Keephub form submissions with OpenAI GPT-4.1

parameters.content:
## 📋 Keephub Form → AI Task Creator

**What it does:**
Automatically creates intelligent follow-up tasks for managers when employees submit forms. AI analyzes the submission and generates a contextual task with relevant fields—no manual work required.

**How it works:**
1. Employee submits any Keephub form
2. Workflow identifies their manager (parent org node)
3. AI analyzes submission data and designs an appropriate task
4. Task is created and assigned to the manager

**Quick Setup:**
1. **Credentials:** Connect Keephub Login (4 nodes), Keephub Bearer (Create Task), and OpenAI
2. **Config:** Set `groupId` to target specific user groups (e.g., only managers)
3. **Test:** Submit a form and watch the magic happen

**Pro tip:** Works with ANY Keephub form—no per-form configuration needed.

## 🚦 Webhook Filter

**Purpose:** Ensures only NEW form submissions trigger task creation.

**Filters out:**
• Status changes (approvals/rejections)
• Edits to existing submissions
• Any other webhook noise

**Result:** Clean, predictable workflow execution.

## 🔍 Data Collection & Target Resolution

**1. Extract Form Data**
Normalizes all field types (NodeSelector, Radio, Checkbox, Date, etc.) into a clean, indexed structure.

**2. Get Submitter**
Fetches the employee's profile and organizational unit memberships.

**3. Resolve Org Node**
Determines which org node the submission belongs to:
• Uses NodeSelector field value (if present in form)
• Falls back to submitter's primary orgunit
• Flexible: supports both explicit and automatic assignment

**4. Get Parent Node**
Finds the manager one level up in the hierarchy (with error handling).

**5. Get Root Node** (fallback)
If no parent exists (submitter is at/near top), fetches the actual organizational root to prevent targeting errors.

**6. Get Form Schema**
Retrieves field labels and structure for building human-readable AI prompts.

**Result:** Clean context ready for AI analysis.

## 🤖 AI-Powered Task Generation

**1. Build AI Prompt**
Creates a structured prompt with:
• Form title and all submitted field values
• Task design rules (language, field types, validation)
• Instructions for contextual, actionable output

💡 **Customization tip:** For single-form use, edit the prompt in "Build AI Prompt" node to specify exactly which fields you need.

**2. Design Task with AI**
GPT-4.1 analyzes the submission and generates:
• Context-aware task title (references specifics from form)
• Manager instructions paragraph
• 3-6 actionable form fields (RadioButtons, TextArea, DatePicker, etc.)

**3. Compose Task**
• Validates AI output (checks for required fields, malformed data)
• Generates cryptographically strong field IDs
• URL-encodes link back to original submission
• Builds full Keephub API payload with dates, targeting, and permissions

**4. Create Task**
Posts to Keephub API. Mission accomplished. ✅

## ⚙️ Configuration

Edit these values in the **⚙️ Config** node:

**`groupId`** (string, recommended)
Keephub group ID to target (e.g., "65f8a3b2c1234567890abcde").
• **With groupId:** Task assigned only to users in that group
• **Without groupId:** Task assigned to EVERYONE on the parent node
• Find group IDs in Keephub admin panel

**`taskDueDaysFromNow`** (number, default: 1)
Days from now until task is due.
• Must be ≥ 0 (validated)
• Examples: 1 = tomorrow, 7 = next week

**`workflow2WebhookUrl`** (string, optional)
Webhook URL to chain a follow-up workflow.
• Attached as hookurl to created task
• Leave empty if not needed

## ⚠️ Critical: Group Targeting

**Without `groupId` → task assigned to EVERYONE**
Managers, employees, interns, contractors—all users on the parent node will see the task.

**To target specific groups (recommended):**
1. Open Keephub admin panel
2. Navigate to Groups section
3. Copy the 24-character group ID (e.g., "65f8a3b2c1234567890abcde")
4. Paste into `groupId` field in **⚙️ Config** node

**Why manual configuration?**
Keephub API returns groups as opaque IDs with no human-readable names. Automatic detection would be unreliable and could cause targeting errors.

**Invalid `groupId` = workflow fails** (validates format to prevent silent broadcasting).
